items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Sultanpur Jilla Sahkari Bank Ltd., Uttar Pradesh
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 19, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹3 lakh (Rupees Three Lakh only) on Sultanpur Jilla Sahkari Bank Ltd., Uttar Pradesh (the bank) for contravention of the provisions of Section 26A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act) and for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Membership of Credit Information Companies (CICs) by Co-operative Banks’ and ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read w

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Shimoga District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd., Karnataka
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 15, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹1 lakh (Rupees One Lakh only) on The Shimoga District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd., Karnataka (the bank) for contravention of provisions of Section 20 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the BR Act. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) wit

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Hutatma Sahakari Bank Ltd., Walva, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 19, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹5 lakh (Rupees Five Lakh only) on Hutatma Sahakari Bank Ltd., Walva, Maharashtra (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Loans and Advances to Directors, their Relatives, and Firms / Concerns in which they are Interested’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by RBI wit

items.title: RBI Bulletin – June 2026
items.summary: Today, the Reserve Bank released the June 2026 issue of its monthly Bulletin. The Bulletin includes one speech, one article and current statistics. The article is on the State of the Economy. State of the Economy Geopolitical tensions and trade disruptions persisted, despite the recent interim peace deal in West Asia. Amidst the challenging global environment, the Indian economy grew at 7.8 per cent in Q4:2025-26, supported by private consumption and fixed investment. High-frequency indicators during the first two months suggest sustained economic momentum in 2026-27. Despite a pick-up in May,

items.summary: On a review of current and evolving liquidity conditions, it has been decided to conduct a Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, as under: Sl. No. Notified Amount (₹ crore) Tenor (day) Window Timing Date of Reversal 1 2,00,000 7 09:30 AM to 10:00 AM June 30, 2026 (Tuesday) 2. items.title: SEBI proposes consolidating technolgy norms across MIIs
items.summary: SEBI plans unified IT framework for exchanges, clearing corporations and depositories

items.title: 2-year Treasury note yield hits highest since February 2025
items.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose four basis points, to 4.491%.

items.title: AMFI reshuffle expected to see a marginal rise in large-cap cut-off, drop in mid-cap bar
items.summary: Quality stocks lift large-cap bar; mid-cap eligibility eases amid market divergence

items.title: Governance concerns need not involve fraud to erase market value: InGovern on Kaynes
items.summary: Repeated guidance cuts, disclosure lapses and weak cash conversion undermined investor confidence despite 33% revenue growth and an ₹8,000 crore order book

items.title: Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp as Meta takes a bite of Cred
items.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has named fintech entrepreneur Kunal Shah as the new global head of WhatsApp, pulling off an unusual executive crossover as part of a $900 million investment in his financial services startup Cred

items.title: Vance hails 'great progress' in U.S.-Iran talks despite 'threatening' and 'whining'
items.summary: JD Vance said U.S.-Iran talks in Switzerland made progress, with Tehran agreeing to allow IAEA inspectors back into Iran.

items.title: RBI intervenes with $8.94 billion forex sales as rupee weakens; gold holdings unchanged
items.summary: The rupee weakened sharply to a record low of 96.96 per dollar, driven by rising oil prices, higher global bond yields and geopolitical tensions linked to the US-Iran conflict

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham? And 4 other things investors should know as the U.K. replaces its prime minister
items.summary: Keir Starmer’s exit puts Andy Burnham, gilts, sterling and the next U.K. chancellor in focus for investors.

items.title: India’s wheat procurement by Govt tops 35.5 million tonnes target
items.summary: Purchases in Rajasthan at record high, UP buys double from a year ago

items.title: Treasury Department authorizes Iranian oil sales through August
items.summary: Iranian supertankers have switched on their transponders, after going dark during the war, as they depart the Persian Gulf loaded with oil.

items.title: India-bound fertilizer ships cross Hormuz, government says
items.summary: Four cargo ships laden with vital fertilizers like urea and di-ammonium phosphate have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, en route to Indian ports. This development significantly bolsters the nation's fertilizer reserves, crucial for meeting the upcoming summer crop season's demands. India continues its robust import strategy, supplementing domestic production to ensure ample crop nutrient availability for farmers nationwide.

items.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
items.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes

items.title: India deserves a different valuation framework in the current regime, says Delorean Partners' Amit Bhartia
items.summary: Amit Bhartia suggests India's equity market requires new valuation standards rather than relying on historical averages. He emphasizes the importance of assessing current economic contexts instead of comparing price-to-earnings ratios, cautioning against misleading conclusions drawn from past data.

items.title: Syrma SGS, Kaga Electronics to form manufacturing JV with focus on Japanese clients
items.summary: Syrma SGS will hold a 60 per cent stake in the joint venture company (JVCo), and the rest will be held by the Indian arm of Japanese firm Kaga Electronics.

items.title: SpaceX stock falls 9%, pacing for third-straight day of losses after red-hot IPO start
items.summary: A rally following the company's record breaking IPO on June 12 has cooled, with stock dropping the past two full days of trading.

items.title: As SoH blockade continues, India again pivots towards the US for LPG & LNG in June
items.summary: LPG and LNG imports from the US to India during May 2026 were at record levels, Kpler data show

items.title: Air India brings back ex-CMD Pradeep Kharola amid CEO transition plan
items.summary: In a message to employees on Monday evening, outgoing CEO Campbell Wilson announced that Kharola would join the airline with immediate effect as executive advisor to the chairman and become a full member of Air India's management committee.

items.title: Inflows into gold ETFs turned positive last week after 5 weeks
items.summary: However, there was a $2 exit for every $3 inflow in the week ending June 19, WGC data show

items.title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
items.summary: SpaceX has turned its Colossus data center into a commercial computing power platform, landing recent deals with Anthropic, Google and Cursor.

items.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
items.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.

items.title: Air India appoints former civil aviation secretary as advisor to chairman
items.summary: Air India has appointed former civil aviation secretary Pradeep Singh Kharola as executive advisor to Chairman N Chandrasekaran, aiding in the upcoming CEO transition. Kharola, instrumental in Air India's privatization, will also manage government communications amidst the airline's significant financial losses and operational challenges, including extended flight routes due to airspace closures. This move aims to steer the carrier towards financial recovery.

items.title: Nifty claws back Friday's losses; Rupee stumbles despite softer crude
items.summary: Sensex closed at 77,094.07, up 291.17 points or 0.38%, while the Nifty 50 settled at 24,102.90, a gain of 89.80 points or 0.37%

items.title: Bajaj Auto clears ₹5,633 crore buyback at ₹12,000 per share
items.summary: The buyback will be conducted through the tender offer route on a proportionate basis, using the stock exchange mechanism prescribed under SEBI's Buyback Regulations, 2018

items.title: RBI sold net $8.9 billion in April, gold holdings unchanged
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India engaged vigorously in the forex market in April, offloading a net amount of $8.94 billion to protect the rupee from plunging to record lows caused by the U.S.-Iran crisis and increasing oil costs. While gold stocks stayed stable, improving geopolitical conditions have bolstered the rupee's prospects, even as forex reserves fell to the lowest level in a year.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
items.summary: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

items.title: ‘Albania is not for sale’: Protests grow over Kushner-linked luxury development project
items.summary: The primary focus of the unrest is a proposed multi-billion-euro tourism project on Albania's Adriatic coast.

items.title: 'It’s been a minute', tweets Kunal Shah as he is appointed WhatsApp's global CEO by Meta— Who is he?
items.summary: Hours after Meta announced CRED founder Kunal Shah as WhatsApp's new boss, the entrepreneur shared a post looking back on his journey from exiting FreeCharge to building one of India's most valuable fintech startups.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium Metal to double production capacity to 60 LTPA
items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium Metal Ltd, now a listed entity, plans to double its production capacity to 60 lakh tonnes per annum. This expansion aims to meet surging demand from infrastructure, automotive, and electrification sectors. With existing facilities in Odisha and Chhattisgarh, and significant captive resources, the company is set to become a global leader in low-cost, high-quality aluminium production, serving over 60 countries.

items.title: SpaceX makes bond sale days after record IPO, discloses over $100 billion cash pile
items.summary: SpaceX on Monday unveiled a senior unsecured notes offering and said it held about $100.8 billion in cash.

items.title: TCS to kickstart Q1 earnings on July 9, sets record date for potential dividend
items.summary: TCS will kick off the Q1FY27 earnings season on July 9, with its board set to consider an interim dividend alongside quarterly results. Investors will closely track management commentary on AI-driven demand, client spending trends, deal wins, margins and the broader outlook for the IT services sector.

items.title: Global economy remains fragile despite US-Iran truce, but India has buffers to weather shocks: RBI
items.summary: Global economic fragility persists despite a US-Iran truce, with risks of renewed inflation and supply disruptions if tensions re-escalate. However, India remains well-positioned due to strong fundamentals like steady growth and anchored inflation. While a weak monsoon poses a domestic challenge, the nation's external sector shows resilience, supported by robust foreign exchange reserves and consistent investment inflows.

items.title: Chevron to fuel massive Microsoft data center in Texas using natural gas
items.summary: Microsoft's embrace of natural gas shows a willingness to invest in fossil fuels to meet the power demand needed in its data centers.

items.title: Bharat Electronics shares to be in focus on Tuesday as company secures ₹1,081 crore order
items.summary: Bharat Electronics has secured additional orders worth ₹1,081 crore, enhancing its revenue visibility. The company's stock is attracting attention following prior orders and discussions of Indian defense sales to the UAE, contributing to a notable demand increase for India's defense systems.

items.title: US authorises Iranian oil sales until August 21 as part of push for final West Asia truce deal
items.summary: Washington has granted Iran a 60-day waiver on oil sanctions, permitting the nation to produce, export, and sell its crude until August 21. This move, part of an interim deal to de-escalate tensions, offers significant relief to Iran's energy sector. The decision could boost Iranian oil shipments, potentially alleviating global supply worries and stabilizing energy costs.

items.title: Andrew Cuomo to lead new venture between NYSE parent and crypto exchange OKX
items.summary: Former New York governor’s role highlights how politicians are rushing into digital assets sector

items.title: India achieves MIV 2030 ship-recycling goal ahead of schedule
items.summary: India has surged to become the world's leading ship-recycling nation in 2025, achieving this milestone ahead of schedule. This significant growth, driven by policy reforms and adherence to international standards, saw India's share rise to 35.4%.

items.title: RBI flags rising fuel-price pressures amid West Asia war, says crude is easing as Hormuz fears recede
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has highlighted rising fuel prices and a dip in consumer confidence due to West Asian conflict, despite a recent drop in crude oil costs. While inflation edged up in May, driven by broad-based price increases, the Indian economy remains resilient with sustained activity. However, manufacturing firms are experiencing higher input costs, impacting production.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Dow tick higher as investors weigh Mideast negotiations
items.summary: Wall Street's major indexes opened little changed on Monday as investors returned from the long holiday weekend and awaited clarity on US-Iran negotiations,

items.title: Shipping stalls in Strait of Hormuz after Iran declares key waterway closed again
items.summary: The update comes even as industry trackers showed Iranian tankers have continued to sail through the strait.

items.title: Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp as Meta invests ₹8,550 crore in the fintech startup
items.summary: Meta Platforms has appointed Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp following its ₹8,550 crore investment in fintech startup Cred.

items.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
items.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.

items.title: So long Starmer, markets found you dull
items.summary: A market obituary in three charts

items.title: 117-year-old bourse Calcutta Stock Exchange eyeing a revival under new Bengal govt
items.summary: The West Bengal government is exploring a revival of the 117-year-old Calcutta Stock Exchange, which has remained dormant since 2013. Officials are evaluating alternative business models and engaging stakeholders to restore the historic bourse, while addressing regulatory and legal challenges that have stalled its operations for years.

items.title: India's investor base tops 130 million, but only one in 10 trade
items.summary: Of the 131 million registered investors, only 12.9 million traded at least once in May across equity cash and derivatives segments.

items.title: US stocks: Oaktree-backed ITG targets $2.7 billion valuation in US IPO
items.summary: Digital infrastructure firm ITG is aiming for a valuation of up to $2.67 billion in its US stock market debut, seeking to raise as much as $429 million. The company, which supports broadband network construction and maintenance, is capitalizing on strong AI-driven demand and a busy summer for IPOs. ITG's services are crucial for high-speed connectivity, with major clients like Comcast and Charter Communications contributing significantly to its revenue.

items.title: CISF to integrate facial recognition data from major airports
items.summary: The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) is planning a nationwide data fusion centre in Delhi to integrate facial recognition data from major airports. This initiative, currently under Home Ministry consideration, aims to enhance security by linking approximately 1.5 lakh CCTV cameras. The force is also expanding its security mandate to seaports and developing a hybrid security model utilizing trained Private Security Assistants.

items.title: Keir Starmer turned India-UK FTA into his tenure defining deal. Here's how
items.summary: Keir Starmer's Labour government, which secured a landslide victory, prioritized strengthening ties with India, culminating in the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) set to take effect on July 15. This landmark deal aims to significantly boost bilateral trade. Starmer's premiership also focused on rebuilding public confidence and repairing relations with the British Indian community, promising a "new strategic partnership" with India.

items.title: India becomes world's largest ship recycling nation ahead of 2030 target
items.summary: India has surged to become the world's leading ship recycler, surpassing its Maritime India Vision 2030 target. Ship recycling volume jumped significantly in 2025, boosting India's global share to over 35%. This achievement solidifies the nation's role as a key player in responsible and sustainable ship dismantling, with ambitious plans to further expand capacity at the Alang Ship Recycling Yard to meet growing global demand.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip as US-Iran talks ease oil prices
items.summary: US stock futures were mixed on June 22 amidst uncertainty over Middle East peace talks. S&P 500 futures fell 0.2%, while the Dow dipped 29 points. Investors are cautious as tech stocks pause after gains, awaiting clarity on US-Iran diplomatic efforts.

items.title: Tencent tests AI assistant in China's most popular app as it looks to catch up with rivals
items.summary: WeChat is an indispensable part of daily life in China and Tencent is trying to tap into its huge user base to expand use of its AI services.

items.title: Core sector output slows to seven-month low of 0.5% in May
items.summary: India's core sector growth hit a seven-month low of 0.5% in May, primarily due to a significant slump in energy-related industries. Petroleum products saw their sharpest contraction in 42 months, while coal output also declined. However, robust growth in electricity generation, driven by high temperatures and renewable energy, prevented a steeper fall. Cement and steel sectors showed steady expansion, supported by infrastructure development.

items.title: UK PM Starmer resigns as Britain faces its seventh leader in 10 years
items.summary: Keir Starmer said he will resign as U.K. prime minister and Labour leader, triggering a contest to choose his successor.

items.title: US stocks: Miner Sinda kicks off US IPO roadshow with $1.97 billion valuation target
items.summary: Silver miner Sinda has launched its US IPO, aiming for a valuation up to $1.97 billion and seeking to raise $235.2 million. This debut adds to a busy summer for new stock market listings, fueled by strong equity markets. The company, backed by The Electrum Group and led by Thomas Kaplan, is developing a significant silver deposit in Mexico.

items.title: The budget airline model in the U.S. is running out of runway
items.summary: Spirit's bankruptcy as United and Delta fly high suggests there's more to success in airline industry than low fares, and failure isn't about jet fuel spikes.

items.title: US stocks: SpaceX launches debt sale to raise capital, reports $100.8 billion in cash
items.summary: SpaceX is making waves with a new notes offering, and they've revealed a massive $100.8 billion cash balance. This significant financial position suggests the company is well-funded for its ambitious future plans, including further Starship development and its ongoing Starlink satellite internet expansion.

items.title: Sebi proposes sweeping revamp of technology rules for stock exchanges
items.summary: Sebi has proposed a major revamp of technology and cyber security rules for stock exchanges, clearing corporations and depositories. The regulator aims to simplify regulations, eliminate overlaps, strengthen cyber resilience and improve ease of doing business through a unified framework covering audits, capacity planning, disaster recovery and algo trading.

items.title: World Bank approves USD 1.5 bn loan to support India's reforms, job creation
items.summary: India's push for private sector-led growth and job creation has received a significant boost with the World Bank approving USD 1.5 billion in financing. This funding will support structural reforms aimed at simplifying business regulations, enhancing trade, and improving access to capital. The initiative is projected to create employment for millions of young Indians and bolster the nation's economic resilience, aligning with the government's long-term development vision.

items.title: India's private investment gains overtakes government share post-COVID: Report
items.summary: Private companies have emerged as the main drivers of investment in India after the pandemic, accounting for 71.3% of project announcements between FY23 and FY26, up from 54.2% in the four years before COVID-19, according to a Bank of Baroda report citing CMIE data.

items.title: Quote of the day by Chris Browne: "In a world that thrives on 24 hours a day financial news, inactivity is seen as brain dead"
items.summary: Chris Browne’s quote highlights the importance of patience in investing amid constant market noise. The piece explains how excessive trading, emotional decisions and information overload can hurt returns, while disciplined, long-term strategies focused on fundamentals and valuation help investors build sustainable wealth over time.

items.title: Crude tankers return to Strait of Hormuz amid a tentative recovery in oil traffic
items.summary: Oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz saw an uptick on Monday, with four vessels carrying nearly 2 million barrels of crude navigating the crucial waterway. This follows a dip in Sunday's flows amid passage concerns. While traffic is increasing, it remains significantly below pre-war levels, indicating ongoing caution in the region.

items.title: Axis Bank hires McKinsey veteran as AI officer to drive enterprise-wide push
items.summary: Namrata Dubashi joins as AI chief to lead a 50-member team and integrate the technology across the bank’s operations.

items.title: MSMEs nursery of entrepreneurship, key to Viksit Bharat vision: RBI Guv Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra lauded MSMEs as crucial for India's growth, highlighting their role in entrepreneurship and employment. He noted strong credit growth in Kerala's MSME sector and detailed RBI's initiatives to bridge credit gaps, including enhanced collateral-free loans and the Trade Receivables Discounting System. Banks were urged to partner with MSMEs, with the upcoming Unified Lending Interface set to simplify credit access.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: Nasdaq slides over 1% as investors weigh Iran talks; SpaceX plunges nearly 10%
Sri Priyanka Geo Commex’s ₹94.5-crore IPO opens on Wednesday at NSE-SME
items.summary: The IPO offers up to 44.58 lakh new shares at a price band of ₹207-212, closing on June 29

items.title: Gold, silver rebound as weak rupee, global rally revive bullion demand
items.summary: Gold, with 99.9% purity, ended its four-day losing streak, rising ₹1,700 to ₹1,52,300 per 10 grams; Silver also increased by ₹4,800 to ₹2,45,500 per kilogram, up from ₹2,40,700

items.title: India bonds steady as dicey Iran truce, liquidity crunch curb appetite
items.summary: Indian government bonds remained steady as traders adopted a cautious stance due to lingering U.S.-Iran tensions and tight banking liquidity. While oil prices dipped, offering some relief, market participants anticipate a period of consolidation after recent gains. Foreign inflows into bonds are strong, though rising U.S. Treasury yields present a potential risk. A key upcoming event is Bloomberg's decision on including Indian bonds in its Global Aggregate Index.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
items.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

items.title: India's manufacturing export boom: Why Mukul Kochhar says the time is 'right now'
items.summary: India's economic outlook is brightening, with a top strategist predicting a multi-year manufacturing export boom. Despite past currency woes and foreign investor jitters, the nation's macro picture has strengthened, evidenced by a neutral current account. Crucially, new trade agreements and competitive energy costs are positioning Indian manufacturers to vie for global market share, shifting the focus from import substitution to international integration.

items.title: Inflationary pressures, reduction in discretionary cash flows may impact collection of MFIs: Report
items.summary: Microfinance borrowers' repayment behavior may face challenges from inflation and reduced household cash, though the West Asia conflict hasn't directly impacted the sector. El Nino's effect on rural incomes also warrants attention. While Karnataka saw collection dips after new regulations, Tamil Nadu and Bihar adapted effectively. Stricter lending guardrails have significantly improved securitised loan pool performance, reducing overdue build-up by limiting exposure to over-leveraged borrowers.

items.title: Over 400 ships are waiting near Hormuz for the strait to reopen fully
items.summary: Many tanker operators reluctant to move vessels until Iran and the US strengthen ceasefire deal

items.title: India's core sector output slows to 0.5% in May as against 1.8% in April
items.summary: India's core industrial output saw a significant slowdown in May 2026, registering a mere 0.5% growth compared to the previous year, a dip from April's 1.8%. While steel, cement, and electricity production showed positive momentum, the overall performance of the eight key sectors, including coal and refinery products, indicates a cautious economic pace. This cumulative slowdown for April-May 2026-27 now stands at 1.1%.

items.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
items.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.

items.title: Assembly Luggage: Redefining Premium Travel, Designed in India
items.summary: Assembly is redefining premium travel gear by designing in India for practical Indian travel realities. Catering to over 103,000 travellers, their durable, stylish luggage, from trolleys to backpacks, meets strict cabin limits and family travel needs. Achieving accessible pricing by selling direct, Assembly offers world-class, homegrown travel solutions, with plans for further retail expansion.

items.title: South East Water announces new chief executive
items.summary: John Halsall has previously worked for Thames Water, South West Water and Network Rail.

items.title: Learn the business, and your value as an HR partner grows exponentially: Coromandel's Arun Leslie George
items.summary: Having turned around businesses and managed P&Ls before returning to HR, Arun Leslie George, President and CHRO of Coromandel International, says learning the business deeply is what makes HR leaders truly effective partners.

items.title: Kirloskar Oil Engines among 5 capital goods stocks hit 52-week highs, rally up to 45% in a month
items.summary: Five capital goods stocks, led by Kirloskar Oil Engines, touched fresh 52-week highs on Monday, delivering gains of up to 45% over the past month amid strong market momentum.

items.title: Renault ships first batch of India-made Duster SUVs to South Africa
items.summary: Renault India has commenced exporting its all-new Duster SUV, with an initial shipment of 750 units dispatched to South Africa. This move underscores India's growing importance as a manufacturing and export hub for the French automaker, aligning with its "futuREady" strategy.

items.title: India's first coal-to-ammonium nitrate plant anchors ₹47,000 crore Odisha infra push
Jio Platforms flags talent retention as a key risk to IPO success
items.summary: Jio Platforms warns that losing skilled personnel in AI, cybersecurity and network engineering could harm its business.

items.title: Central Drugs Laboratories find 46 drug samples not of standard quality for May
items.summary: Health officials revealed that in May 2026, 46 drug samples tested by Central Drugs Laboratories and 113 by State Drugs Testing Laboratories were found to be of substandard quality. Additionally, one spurious drug, manufactured by an unauthorized entity using another company's brand, was identified in Assam and is under investigation. These findings highlight ongoing efforts to ensure drug safety and remove substandard products from circulation.

items.title: IPO-bound Laxyo expands operations in Africa; bags Rs 200-crore order in Zambia
items.summary: Indore-based Laxyo Ltd has significantly expanded its global footprint by securing a substantial Rs 200-crore raise-boring contract in Zambia. This marks the company's inaugural overseas project in this specialized mining technique, contributing significantly to its order book. The move positions Laxyo among international industry leaders and is a key step in its international growth strategy.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex rises 291 points, Nifty above 24,100; markets rebound on global cues
items.summary: Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets rebounded on Monday, with Sensex rising 291 points and Nifty gaining 89.80 points, driven by easing crude prices, strong global cues, foreign inflows and hopes of progress in US–Iran talks, even as the rupee weakened against the dollar.

items.title: HUL Share Price Live Updates: HUL achieves notable returns in the last quarter
Rupee snaps six-session winning streak as firmer dollar pinches
items.summary: The Indian rupee slipped on Monday, snapping its six-day upward trend due to the strengthening dollar worldwide. While falling oil prices eased some apprehensions related to conflicts, the Federal Reserve's assertive monetary policy continues to support the dollar. Consequently, traders are bracing for further upticks in U.S. interest rates, which is hindering the rupee's potential recovery, despite initiatives to boost dollar inflows.

items.title: India imposes anti-dumping duty on chemical imported from China, EU, US
items.summary: India has slapped a five-year anti-dumping duty on a key chemical used in rubber and tyre manufacturing, imported from China, the EU, and the US. This move follows a recommendation by the Directorate General of Trade Remedies, citing dumped imports causing material injury to domestic producers. Additional duties have also been extended on Aluminium Foil and imposed on Polyethylene Terephthalate resin from China, aiming to ensure fair trade.

Green residential developments can reduce water consumption by 30-50%: Report
items.summary: India's burgeoning green building sector faces a transparency gap, hindering homebuyers' ability to assess sustainability. To address this, Square Yards has launched the Green Living Index, a standardized framework to measure and compare residential project credentials. This initiative aims to empower consumers with clear, data-driven insights into energy efficiency, water conservation, and overall environmental impact, crucial for India's rapid urbanization and Net-Zero goals.

Nayara Energy crosses 7,000 petrol pumps in India
items.summary: Nayara Energy, backed by Russia's Rosneft, has surpassed 7,000 petrol pumps nationwide, solidifying its status as India's leading private fuel retailer. The company has rapidly expanded its network, adding over 500 outlets in just 18 months, with a significant presence in rural areas. This growth not only enhances fuel accessibility but also supports over 55,000 livelihoods through its dealer partnerships, aligning with its 'In India for India' vision.

items.title: Coal India Share Price Live Updates: Coal India Experiences Slight Price Drop
Petrol, diesel margins back to above pre-conflict levels: Report
items.summary: State-run oil companies are poised for improved profitability as falling crude prices boost fuel marketing margins. While recent government excise duty cuts have aided this recovery, rising debt levels and potential tax increases pose risks to long-term earnings. Analysts anticipate better performance from the second quarter onwards, especially if oil prices remain below $80 per barrel, with BPCL and IOC seen as preferred investments.

items.title: Minority shareholders ask Supreme Court to revive Jindal Poly class action lawsuit
items.summary: A group of 29 investors has sought the recall of the Supreme Court's arbitration order, arguing India’s first corporate class action suit was settled without hearing all shareholders represented in the case.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 22: Kirloskar Oil Engines, Delhivery, Gabriel India, Urban Company among top gainers
items.summary: The Indian stock market closed with gains as US-Iran peace talks progressed, boosting pharmaceutical and technology stocks. The Nifty 50 rose 0.37% to 24,102, while the Sensex closed at 77,044, up 0.31%. Broader markets and various sector indices also recorded positive movements.

items.title: Akasa Air adds Boeing 737 MAX aircraft to its fleet, airline's 39th plane
items.summary: Akasa Air welcomed its 39th Boeing 737 MAX aircraft, a significant addition to its fleet this year. The new plane, VT-YBP, completed its delivery journey from Seattle to Bengaluru. With 187 more aircraft on order over six years, the airline is expanding its network, currently serving 27 domestic and 7 international routes, signaling robust growth.

items.title: Can NSE IPO, Jio Platforms IPO impact liquidity in the Indian stock market?
items.summary: Jio Platforms and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) are set to launch major IPOs, raising billions. Jio's IPO could reach $4 billion, and the NSE aims for around ₹30,000 crore, raising concerns about liquidity impacts on the secondary market.

items.title: Indian Railways approves Kavach deployment worth Rs 270 crore on 631 route kms
items.summary: Indian Railways has approved the installation of Kavach, an indigenous automatic train protection system, on 631 route kilometers of East Coast Railway for Rs 270 crore. This move aims to significantly boost railway safety by preventing collisions and over-speeding. The expansion covers crucial sections in Odisha and neighboring areas, enhancing train operations even in adverse weather and reinforcing the mission to deploy Kavach across vital routes.

items.summary: Power Mech Projects shares rose 5% after securing a ₹1,008.90 crore order from JSW Thermal Energy for civil and structural works in West Bengal. The contract, to be completed in 36 months, enhances the company’s order book amid a series of recent large contract wins.

items.title: Should you buy BSE shares ahead of the NSE IPO?
items.summary: NSE IPO is expected to be among the largest IPOs in Indian stock market history, with estimates pegging the issue size at around ₹30,000 crore. Reports suggest the NSE IPO could be priced in the range of ₹1,800 - ₹2,040 per share.

items.title: Resonia wins Telangana transmission project to boost renewable energy connectivity
items.summary: Resonia, formerly Sterlite Power's transmission arm, has secured a significant project in Telangana from Power Finance Corporation. This initiative aims to bolster power transmission links between Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, improving grid flexibility and facilitating the integration of renewable energy from the Kurnool REZ. The project involves establishing a new substation and enhancing existing infrastructure to support this crucial connectivity.

items.title: Platinum, palladium lose glitter, but may gain by the end of Q3
items.summary: Platinum and palladium prices may return to levels above $2,000 an ounce (oz) and $1,500/oz, respectively, but if Iran war continues they may drop another 10%

items.title: ArcelorMittal partners with AWS to automate global operations
items.summary: Steel giant ArcelorMittal is joining forces with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to revolutionize its global operations. By integrating AWS's cloud and AI capabilities, the company aims to enhance safety, improve asset reliability, and boost energy efficiency across its manufacturing processes. This strategic partnership will leverage advanced technologies like industrial IoT and machine learning to enable predictive maintenance and optimize production, marking a significant step towards digitally enabled, sustainable steelmaking.

items.title: Diksha Polymers IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, how to check status
items.summary: The shares of Diksha Polymer IPO is currently trading ₹0 in the grey market, according to websites tracking the unlisted market. The GMP of Diksha Polymer IPO is +0, as per Investorgain.

items.title: For Tata Capital, narrowing profitability gap with Bajaj Finance is a tough task
items.summary: Tata Capital has hit a record high after reiterating its FY28 growth roadmap. But with much of the optimism already priced in, investors may need to temper expectations.

items.title: Primus Partners appoints Mohan Doifode as managing director for its Middle East practice
items.summary: Primus Partners has appointed Mohan Doifode as Managing Director for its Middle East operations, based in Dubai and Riyadh. With over 25 years of experience, including roles at Deloitte and founding Meraki Global, Doifode will spearhead the firm's growth strategy in the GCC. His expertise in strategy, transformation, and public policy will enhance client engagement and consulting services across the region, reinforcing Primus Partners' international expansion.

items.title: K2 Infra bags Rs 390.91 crore Telangana road project
items.summary: K2 Infra Limited has bagged a significant Rs 390.91 crore road project from the Telangana government. This initiative focuses on enhancing rural road connectivity in the Bhongir Circle, aiming to improve travel for residents, businesses, and essential services. The project involves construction, upgradation, and maintenance of selected PRED roads under a dedicated rural development program.

items.title: The smartphone is becoming India’s most important financial advisor
items.summary: India's financial landscape is rapidly digitizing, with smartphones becoming central to everyday money management. Once separate, banking and investment activities now converge on single platforms, making financial planning more accessible. This shift, driven by convenience and proximity, empowers millions entering formal finance to make informed decisions, transforming income into resilience and savings into wealth. The smartphone is emerging as a key guide in this evolution.

items.title: IIT Roorkee, IvyCap Ventures and NuQuant announce Rs 1,000 crore Super Endowment initiative to strengthen India’ s research, innovation and DeepTech ecosystem
items.summary: IIT Roorkee, in partnership with IvyCap Ventures and NuQuant, has launched a Rs 1,000 crore Super Endowment Fund to fuel India's deep-tech innovation. This perpetual fund will support research, entrepreneurship, and technology translation across the IIT network, fostering global collaborations in strategic sectors. The initiative aims to create a self-sustaining ecosystem, empowering the next generation of innovators and entrepreneurs.

items.title: Penny stock under ₹5 hits upper circuit after announcement to move into digital solar solutions market
items.summary: Empower India share price increased by 5% following its announcement to expand into next-generation digital solar solutions. This initiative aims to overcome challenges in solar adoption and supports India's clean energy transition.

items.summary: Shanti Educational Initiatives opened at ₹207.50 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹203.60 on Friday. The multibagger stock touched an intraday high of ₹220 on 22 June.

items.title: AMFI Categorisation: Vedanta Aluminium may get largecap status; Vedanta Power, Physicswallah, others to enter smallcaps
items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium is likely to qualify as a Large Cap stock under the AMFI categorisation, while Vedanta Power, Vedanta Oil & Gas, and Vedanta Iron & Steel will fall under Small Cap category, according to estimates by Nuvama Alternative Research.

items.title: India's 'fragmented' water management framework raises fiscal, credit risks: Moody's
items.summary: India's water management system faces significant challenges, according to Moody's Ratings. A fragmented governance structure, heavily subsidized water prices, and slow reallocation across sectors are contributing to water shortages and potential fiscal strain. Growing demand from data centers further exacerbates industrial pressure. These issues, coupled with aging infrastructure and groundwater depletion, pose a high credit risk for the nation.

items.title: India seeks tariff advantage over peers in push to finalise US trade deal
items.summary: India's trade minister, Piyush Goyal, revealed on Monday that a trade agreement with the United States is experiencing delays. The primary hurdle, he explained, is the existing 50% tariff imposed on Indian goods. New Delhi is actively seeking preferential market access through this pact, a key objective for the nation's economic strategy.

items.title: Why a 66-year-old water treaty is becoming the latest India-Pakistan flashpoint
items.summary: India and Pakistan are locked in a dispute over the Indus Water Treaty after New Delhi suspended the pact, raising concerns about regional stability.

items.title: Air India partners with Booking. com for integrated travel and stay bookings, offering exclusive rewards
items.summary: Air India has joined forces with Booking. com, a leading online travel agency, to offer a seamless travel booking experience. Through a new co-branded platform on Air India's website and app, customers can now book flights and accommodations together. Maharaja Club members will enjoy exclusive benefits and earn five Maharaja points for every ₹100 spent, enhancing their travel rewards.

items.title: Ambuja Cements ties up with UK's Leilac for commercial-scale low-carbon cement project
items.summary: Ambuja Cements, an Adani Group company, is partnering with UK's Leilac Limited to pilot a groundbreaking low-carbon cement production method at its Gujarat plant. This initiative will test Leilac's innovative carbon capture and electric heating technology, aiming to slash emissions and fuel use. If successful, the project could become the world's largest industrial-scale carbon capture facility, significantly advancing Ambuja's net-zero goals by 2050.

This initiative aims to equip students with modern equipment skills, bridging the gap between academic learning and industry needs. The move supports the company's manufacturing expansion plans in Gujarat, ensuring a skilled workforce for the evolving automotive sector.

items.title: Iran war triggers global race to build oil reserves
items.summary: Vulnerable nations, stung by the Iran war's economic fallout, are boosting oil and gas storage to weather future crises. This global push for reserves could add half a billion barrels of demand. Countries like India and Pakistan are prioritizing stockpiles, learning from China's successful strategy of reducing imports during supply disruptions. This move aims to prevent severe economic distress and insulate economies from volatile energy markets.

items.title: India likely won't export sugar for years as El Nino, ethanol squeeze supply
items.summary: Once as a top sugar exporter, India is now anticipating a three-year stretch of significant export deficits, primarily driven by El Nino's adverse effects on cane cultivation. As ethanol demand rises, farmers are beginning to reassess their planting strategies, with some considering alternative crops. This shift might mark the first occasion in a decade where India turns to sugar imports, altering both domestic and international markets.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Markets feeling that deja vu as Iran deal under strain, once again
items.summary: Less than a week after the U.S. and Iran signed their "memorandum of understanding" aimed at ending the conflict, tensions have flared once again.

items.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.

items.title: Indian deep-tech to see funding surge amid sovereign push: Celesta’s Viswanathan
items.summary: Sovereign investment avenues have gained prominence over the past 12 months amid rising geopolitical tensions. US government’s move to restrict access to Anthropic’s latest frontier models has sparked debate in India on the need to build domestic capabilities in AI, other critical technologies.

items.summary: As with other banks, SBI also has “quite a lot of illiquid assets”, such as a ₹10 trillion mortgage book, on its balance sheet which can be monetised, Chairman CS Setty says.

items.title: Basmati prices may fall 5–10% as Strait of Hormuz closure hits exports
items.summary: "After the interim (US-Iran) peace deal was announced, exporters started buying basmati rice in good quantities for shipment to the Middle East and prices surged 15-20%. But this sudden announcement will bring down prices," said Dev Garg, vice-president, Indian Rice Exporters Federation.

items.title: Nearly 12 ships carrying fertiliser cross Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: At the onset of the Iran war, eight urea, four diammonium phosphate (DAP), one ammonia and three sulphur vessels bound for India were stranded in the strait.

items.title: PSBs told to tighten scrutiny of government accounts amid fraud concerns
items.summary: In response to a staggering ₹590 crore fraud uncovered in Haryana government accounts, the finance ministry has mandated that public sector banks must enhance their security measures. Banks are urged to conduct thorough reviews of their current practices, adopt more stringent Standard Operating Procedures, and strengthen security protocols to avert any future fraud incidents.

items.title: FTA map: Mega FTA wave to bring 60% of India trade under preferential regime
items.summary: With the UK deal kicking in next month, an EU pact likely next year, and a US agreement in the works, over 60% of India’s trade will soon fall under preferential terms.

items.title: UK FTA calls for standards upgrade: Experts
items.summary: Indian exporters must elevate product standards and meet UK regulations to fully leverage the upcoming India-UK free trade agreement, effective July 15. Experts urge the government to launch nationwide outreach programs, mirroring UK efforts, to equip domestic industries. This proactive approach will help businesses transform the agreement's potential into tangible commercial success, establishing them as dependable supply chain partners.

items.title: Exports up 15% despite global headwinds: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's merchandise exports saw a robust 15% year-on-year growth in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. This positive trend continues despite US tariff impositions. Meanwhile, crucial trade talks with the US are set to commence this week, aiming to finalize a bilateral trade agreement framework. Discussions will address tariffs and potential new probes.

items.summary: Traders see more volatility ahead as new central bank chair declines to give dot plot on future path of interest rates

items.title: Pixar's 'Toy Story 5' lassos biggest opening weekend in franchise history with $160 million haul
items.summary: Disney and Pixar's "Toy Story 5" tallied $160 million domestically during its opening weekend, the highest debut in franchise history.

items.title: CCPA fines Storia Foods, Mrs Bectors ₹1 lakh each over ‘100%’ claims
items.summary: The CCPA also directs both companies to immediately discontinue the disputed claims from product packaging, websites and digital platforms.

items.title: She saved $24,000 to launch a craft festival in an old Joann store: 'This is my dream come true'
items.summary: "This is my dream come true," says Tetef, a long-time events producer. "I just want to go to a thing where everyone's sitting down making stuff."

items.title: Zypp Electric aims to double its valuation in a $40-50 million pre-IPO round
items.summary: Zypp Electric was last valued at approximately $331 million as of March 2025

items.title: CS Setty says SBI deserves a better valuation. The market is starting to agree.
items.summary: State Bank of India is now better positioned from a regulatory and growth-capital perspective, Chairman C.S. Setty says.

items.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
items.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.

items.title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
items.summary: The first big announced changes point toward a quiet revolution, with task forces set up to rethink virtually everything done at the Fed.

items.title: Will Burnham’s by-election win put pressure on gilts?
items.summary: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

items.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
items.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

items.title: India remains ADB's largest private sector market; lender eyes USD 1 bn direct support in 2026
items.summary: India stands at the forefront of the Asian Development Bank's investment strategies as the lender aims to deploy roughly USD 1 billion in direct private sector support this year. ADB's Vice-President Bhargav Dasgupta highlighted the intention to carry forward last year's progress, particularly within renewable energy and sustainable development sectors.

items.title: India, US to hold ministerial-level talks on bilateral trade pact this week
items.summary: In a significant development, India and the US are set to conclude the first phase of their trade agreement this week. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is scheduled to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, with discussions geared towards finalizing an interim deal before the July 24 deadline, coinciding with the expiration of US temporary tariffs.

items.title: Bharat Taxi eyes 55-60 smaller cities, puts bikes and autos at centre of growth
items.summary: The platform said its focus on smaller cities aligns with its goal of democratising mobility in regions where transport options remain limited. Unlike profit-driven ride-hailing platforms, Bharat Taxi believes its zero-commission model enables sustainable operations.

items.title: India-EU trade pact to be signed by Dec; implemented from Feb-Mar next year: Goyal
items.summary: India is set to sign a free trade agreement with the European Union by December, with implementation expected by February-March next year. This pact promises near duty-free access for about 93% of Indian exports to the EU market. Discussions are also progressing with the US and Canada for similar trade deals, highlighting India's growing global trade significance.

items.title: Exports rise 15 per cent in Apr-Jun 14 despite global uncertainties, says Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's merchandise exports surged approximately 15% in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He highlighted robust growth despite significant US tariffs, with May exports reaching a six-month high. Goyal also urged chartered accountants to contribute to a 'Viksit Bharat,' emphasizing local cleanliness and ethical business practices as foundational steps.

items.title: Mint Explainer| Why Delhivery's homegrown maps matter for e-commerce
items.summary: Delhivery Maps, which was developed to power Delhivery’s own operations, will now be made available to enterprises, developers, and gig-economy companies as a commercial product. Why does this matter for e-commerce?

items.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
items.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.

items.title: RBI expected to hold repo at 5.25% as uncertainty eases, will watch food and fuel pass-through: BofA Securities
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its wait-and-watch stance and keep the repo rate at 5.25 per cent in coming meetings as the US-Iran peace agreement reduces geopolitical uncertainty, while it monitors food and fuel price pass-through before acting, BofA Securities said in a note on the June MPC minutes.

items.title: Beyond Holiday Inn: IHG sees luxury brands, franchises as growth drivers
items.summary: IHG Hotels plans to grow from 52 current hotels to 400 by 2030, driven by infrastructure and owner confidence in the hospitality sector.

items.title: Weak start to monsoon seen clouding Kharif season outlook: Report
items.summary: India's monsoon has begun with a significant rainfall deficit, impacting kharif sowing, particularly for pulses and cotton. While reservoir levels offer a buffer, a prolonged dry spell could raise food prices and strain rural economies. Experts emphasize close monitoring, as the July-August period will be crucial for crop prospects and overall economic recovery.

items.title: SpaceX handed lowest possible ESG rating by MSCI
items.summary: Triple C score puts Elon Musk’s company on par with Russia after 2022 invasion of Ukraine

items.title: India on track for $1 trillion exports in FY27: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India is poised to hit its ambitious $1 trillion export goal by FY27, despite ongoing West Asia challenges, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He expressed strong confidence, citing progress and the impact of free trade agreements and improved business ease. Discussions with the US on a trade pact are finalized, awaiting India's tariff advantage over rivals for implementation.
